dataset: NHANES (synthetic patient if not found in real data)
case_id: Patient PID determined at runtime if exists; else auto-created

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic - created by task if not found in database
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 17-26: Checks for patient existence, creates dummy patient with name 'Jannette Charley' if not found
setup_task.sh line 24: DOB='1965-02-14' used in INSERT for synthetic patient creation
Standard medical terminology verified via NCBI/StatPearls. setup_task.sh line 49 creates order with this exact name
Medical reference: Normal WBC range 4.5-11.0 (thousands/µL). Value 6.8 is within normal range and realistic. See setup_task.sh line 54 units=10*3/uL
Medical reference: Normal RBC for adult women 4.2-5.4 million/µL. Value 4.22 is at lower boundary of normal range. setup_task.sh line 55 units=10*6/uL
Medical reference: Normal hemoglobin for adult women 12.0-16.0 g/dL. Value 11.8 is below normal (anemia threshold <12). Clinically realistic. setup_task.sh line 56
Medical reference: Normal hematocrit for adult women 36-48%. Value 35.4% is below normal, consistent with mild anemia. setup_task.sh line 57
Medical reference: Normal platelet count 150-400 (thousands/µL). Value 245 is within normal range. setup_task.sh line 58
Lab measurement precision for WBC. Value is reasonable for inter-assay variation in clinical labs
Lab measurement precision for RBC in millions. Value is reasonable for measurement repeatability
Lab measurement precision for hemoglobin in g/dL. Value is reasonable for analytical precision in automated hematology analyzers
Lab measurement precision for hematocrit percentage. Value (±1%) is reasonable for clinical lab precision
Lab measurement precision for platelet count (thousands/µL). Value (±10) is reasonable for counting variation

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the 'Jannette Charley' patient (DOB: 1965-02-14), which is a SYNTHETIC patient created by the task setup script if not found in the NHANES database. The patient exhibits mild anemia: WBC 6.8 (normal), RBC 4.22 (low-normal), Hemoglobin 11.8 (below 12 - anemia threshold), Hematocrit 35.4 (below normal 36-48%), and Platelet Count 245 (normal). All target values are clinically realistic with acceptable lab measurement tolerances (±0.5-1.0 for precision). The order name 'Complete Blood Count' is standard medical terminology. Environment loads real NHANES data (9,375 patients) from LibreHealthIO repository, but this specific task creates synthetic test data.
